data.title: FY beat, 'massive jump' in backlog boost Viridien shares
data.description:
** Shares in Viridien jump 9% as the geophysical services company's FY results beat expectations, with a "massive jump" in backlog

** Viridien reports roughly stable y/y FY revenue at $1.12 bln with adj EBITDA up 14% at $455 mln

** Viridien's core geoscience unit posts a 90% jump in backlog, which sits at $351 mln

** Bernstein flags two positive points - the results beat and the "massive jump" in backlog

** There's little colour from the company on the backlog jump, Bernstein says, but contrasted with the CEO's expectations of only modest revenue growth for the segment in 2025 - "this may suggest future good surprises," the brokerage adds

** These provide many short-term positive signals, Bernstein says, also pointing to Viridien's "increasingly conservative" net cash flow target of about $100 mln

** The stock is sitting at the top of France's wider SBF 120 index

data.title: NRW Holdings Sees FY25 Revenue A$3.2 Billion To A$3.3 Billion
data.description:
NRW Holdings Ltd :

  • FY25 REVENUE EXPECTED TO INCREASE TO A$3.2 BILLION TO A$3.3 BILLION

data.title: Northeast Securities Issues 1.50 Billion Yuan in Short-Term Bonds
data.description:
Northeast Securities issued short-term bonds worth 1.50 billion yuan, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange disclosure on Thursday.

The bonds have a 2.05% coupon rate and a term of 178 days, the Chinese securities firm said.

The company's shares closed less than 3% lower.

data.title: Harvest Technology Announces Convertible Note Raising Of A$1.5 Million
data.description:
Harvest Technology Group Ltd :

  • CONVERTIBLE NOTE RAISING OF A$1.5 MILLION

data.title: Fugro jumps on Q4 EBIT beat
data.description:
** Shares in Fugro jump around 8% after the Dutch geological data specialist posted Q4 core profit beat

** Q4 adjusted EBIT came in at 71.8 million euros, ahead of a company-compiled consensus of 54 million euros

**"Return to growth in 4Q24, with better than expected profitability," says Jefferies

** It points to healthy revenue growth of 7% in Europe, and 23% in Asia Pacific, partly offset by lower revenues of 12% in Americas, and 20% in the Middle East

** Fugro also said it will propose a dividend of 0.75 euro per share, up 87.5% year-on-year

** Its shares are on track for their best day since end-September 2024, if gains hold

($1 = 0.9617 euros)

data.title: BCI Minerals Receives Approval For 2nd Phase Of Groundwater Monitoring & Management Plan
data.description:
BCI Minerals Ltd :

  • WA DEPARTMENT OF WATER AND ENVIRONMENTAL REGULATION APPROVED CO'S 2ND PHASE OF GROUNDWATER MONITORING & MANAGEMENT PLAN

data.title: Swiss Business Confidence Drops
data.description:
The KOF Economic Barometer fell to 101.7 in February 2025, down from a revised 103 in January and slightly below market expectations of 102.

Almost all production-side indicators came under pressure, with notable weaknesses in manufacturing and other services.

In manufacturing and construction, stockpiling of intermediate products and overall business sentiment worsened, and assessments of production barriers and profitability weakened slightly.

Within manufacturing, several industries faced setbacks, including the paper and printing, chemicals and pharmaceuticals, wood, glass, stone, and earth materials, as well as machinery and equipment manufacturing.

However, demand-side indicators, particularly those for foreign demand and private consumption, provided a slight cushion against these negative developments.

data.title: Yinson Repurchases More Shares
data.description:
Yinson bought back 425,000 shares for around 944,536 ringgit, according to a Thursday disclosure with Bursa Malaysia.

The shares were repurchased at between 2.180 ringgit and 2.290 ringgit apiece, the energy infrastructure and technology company said.

data.title: Spectris' FY24 Profit Up; Revenue Slips
data.description:
Spectris said Friday its profit for the full year 2024 jumped, while revenue dropped year over year.

Profit attributable to owners of the company for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 233.6 million pounds sterling, compared with 145.4 million pounds a year ago. EPS moved to 2.311 pounds from 1.394 pounds.

The precision instrumentation and controls company's revenue was 1.3 billion pounds, compared with 1.45 billion pounds previously.

Analysts polled by FactSet were expecting 1.27 billion pounds in sales, 148.4 million pounds in net income, and 1.5 pounds in EPS.

Meanwhile, the board will propose a final dividend of 0.566 pound per share, payable on June 27 to shareholders of record on May 16. It paid 0.539 pound per share a year ago. The final dividend takes the full-year 2024 dividend to 0.832 pound per share, up from 0.792 pound per share in 2023.

For 2025, Spectris expects adjusted operating profit to be in line with market expectations.

data.title: Mitrajaya Repurchases More Shares
data.description:
Mitrajaya Holdings bought back 241,200 shares on Thursday for 78,963 ringgit, according to a same-day bourse disclosure.

The shares were purchased at 0.325 ringgit apiece, the Malaysian construction company said.

data.title: Market Chatter: China Stops US IPOs of Small-Cap Firms to Prevent Market Manipulation
data.description:
The China Securities Regulatory Commission has blocked the US initial public offerings of small homegrown companies for alleged price rigging, the Financial Times reported Thursday, citing four people close to the regulator.

The regulator put a stop to the IPOs of small homegrown firms with low capitalization and weak fundamentals as the companies could be prone to market manipulation, the report said, citing the sources.

"Not only the Chinese regulators but also other market participants have questioned why these firms require offshore listings," a source familiar with the regulator's perspective reportedly said.

Chinese approval for US IPOs has slowed in 2024 to 11 in the second half of 2024 from 22 in the first half of the year, the report said.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Eqva ASA: Mandatory notification of trade
data.description:
Even Matre Ellingsen, CEO and primary insider, has on 27 February 2025purchased20 000 shares in Eqva ASA at an average price of NOK 5,4042 per share.

After the transaction, Even Matre Ellingsen controls 8 317 628 shares in EqvaASA, of which 8 297 628 shares are owned by Neve Eiendom AS, and 20 000 sharesare owned directly by Even Matre Ellingsen.

Please see the attached document for further details of the transaction.

This information is subject to the disclosure requirements pursuant to theMarket Abuse Regulation article 19 and 5-12 of the Norwegian SecuritiesTradingAct.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Company News of the Day - Friday at 3 AM ET
data.description:
Allianz Lifts Dividend, Launches Buyback After Rise in Profit

The insurer proposed a dividend increase of 12% for 2024 after net profit rose in the final quarter.

British Airways Owner IAG to Buy Back Shares After Profit Rise

The group said it would buy back around $1 billion in shares after earnings were boosted by commercial and cargo demand.

HP to Trim Up to 2,000 More Jobs in Expanded Cost-Cutting Push

The details of the layoffs came shortly after HP reported higher revenue in its fiscal first quarter, boosted by what Chief Executive Enrique Lores said was strong demand for its new technologies.

KPMG to Launch U.S. Law Firm Following Court Approval

Arizona's Supreme Court gives the firm final clearance to set up a law practice, making KPMG the first Big Four accounting firm to do so anywhere in the U.S.

Dell Stock Falls After AI Server Maker Beats Earnings Estimates

Dell Technologies stock rose after the company posted better-than-expected earnings late Thursday.

Autodesk to Cut 9% of Workforce

The layoffs are expected to hit about 1,350 employees and the software company will incur total restructuring charges between $135 million and $140 million.

OpenAI Unveils New ChatGPT 4.5 Model

ChatGPT Pro users will get access to GPT-4.5 on Thursday as part of a research preview.

Amazon Unveils 'Ocelot' Quantum Chip. It's the Latest Tech Giant to Move Into the Space.

Amazon Web Services introducs the chip in a blog post, coinciding with an article in the journal Nature.

Trump wants a 25% tariff on cars from the E.U. Where he could be going wrong.

Some trade experts haven't been won over by the 47th president's tariff proposals, even as Europe's big car companies are making shifts to deal with Trump 2.0.

Consumer Watchdog Moves to Drop Capital One Lawsuit

President Trump's nominee to lead the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau said he still planned to fulfill the agency's mandate.

data.title: Australia's ASIC Says Allianz And AWP Convicted And Fined
data.description:
Australia's ASIC:

  • ALLIANZ AND AWP CONVICTED AND FINED A$16.8 MILLION FOR MAKING FALSE OR MISLEADING STATEMENTS

  • ALLIANZ AUSTRALIA AND AWP AUSTRALIA WILL ALSO PAY ASIC’S INVESTIGATION COSTS

data.description:
National Bank of Greece SA :

  • FY 2024 NET INTEREST INCOME EUR 2.36 BILLION VERSUS EUR 2.26 BILLION YEAR AGO

  • FY 2024 NPE RATIO AT 2.6%

data.title: Brödernas publishes financial results for the fourth quarter and full year of 2024
data.description:
Brödernas Group AB

February 28, 2025, 09.15 CET

Brödernas Group AB (publ) hereby publishes the report for the fourth quarter (Q4) for the period 1 October – 31 December and full year of 2024. The report is available as an attached document and on the company's website (investors.brodernas.nu).

1 January – 31 December 2024

Net sales amounted to SEK 465m

EBITDA amounted to SEK 13m

1 October – 31 December 2024

Net sales amounted to SEK 111m

EBITDA amounted to SEK 2m

Pursuant to the terms and conditions for Brödernas Group AB (publ)’s senior secured callable fixed rate bonds, it shall ensure that net leverage ratio (net interest bearing debt to EBITDA) does not exceed 6.00x. For the reference period ending 31 December 2024, net leverage ratio (calculated in accordance with the terms and conditions for the bonds) is in excess of 6.00x. In addition to what’s previously being stated, and due to the fact that Brödernas Group AB (publ) is in corporate restructuring proceedings and there are too many uncertainties in the organization to be able to determine the correct financial figures, the publication of the quarterly report does not contain a balance sheet or cash flow analysis, meaning that an event of default has occurred under the bond terms. Complete financial figures for 2024 will be published at latest in connection with the publication of the annual report.

For further information, please contact:

Richard Forsshéll, CEO

press@brodernas.nu 

This information is information that Brödernas Group AB is obliged to make public pursuant to the EU Market Abuse Regulation. The information was submitted, through the provision of the specified contact person, for publication on 28 February 2025 at 09.15 CET.

https://news.cision.com/brodernas-group-ab/r/brodernas-publishes-financial-results-for-the-fourth-quarter-and-full-year-of-2024%2Cc4112460

Brödernas Group AB (publ) - Financial Report Q4 2024
